REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2194 43rd Street, San Francisco Ca 94116)
Off Calendar Nov-21-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Harrington and Daphne Somkin for Franza Giffen.

The Court noted that the petition states 43rd Street but in fact it is 43rd Avenue. Ms. Harrington stated that publication is correct and that the legal description attached to the petition indicates 43rd Avenue. The sale was confirmed at $575,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed October 3, 2013 which along with other terms includes the term "subject to tenant's rights". The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $575,000.00 is required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2194 43rd Street, San Francisco Ca 94116)
Off Calendar Oct-29-2013 Continued To Nov-13-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Petition for Confirmation of Sale of Real Property was continued to November 13, 2013. The matter was called in the event interested buyers/over bidders appeared. No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person. The matter remains continued.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Clark. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1995 44th Ave, San Francisco, Ca 94122)
Off Calendar Oct-15-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Harrington for and with Franza Giffen (Petitioner).

The sale was confirmed at $710,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $175,000.00 is required.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1995 44th Ave, San Francisco, Ca 94122)
Off Calendar Sep-23-2013 Continued To Oct-07-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alicia Gamez made a special appearance for Attorney Amy Harrington for and with Franza Giffen.

The Court noted that the Notice of Hearing now appears to be in order and that we now have a re-appraisal for sale. In addition, the Court stated it does not see something indicating that the Probate Referee was appointed and that appointment of a Prbate Referee is regulated by the Court. The Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property was continued to October 7, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a Probate Referee to be properly appointed.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar May-16-2011 Continued To Jun-27-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any party, counsel or interested person.

Judge Wiss disclosed that Counsel Amy Harrington is the Spouse of a fellow Bench Officer and that she does not feel that this fact will affect her ability to be fair and impartial to all Parties and all Counsel. A Notice to Appear will be issued to the Trustee Franza Giffen and the Attorney Amy Harrington to appear on June 27, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why the first Account has not been filed. The matter was continued to June 27, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos.

Petition For Order Terminating Powers Of Successor Trustee Of Proposed Conservatees Trust And To Appoint Franza Giffen As Trustee Of That Trust; To Br
Off Calendar Feb-08-2010 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Order Terminating Powers Of Successor Trustee Of Proposed Conservatees Trust And To Appoint Franza Giffen As Trustee Of That Trust; To Br
Off Calendar Dec-10-2009 Continued To Feb-08-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number PCN-09-292797.
Appearances: Court-Appointed Counsel Nelson Wild for and with Waneta Averhoff (Conservatee), Nancy Rasch for Debra Dolch (Conservator), Amy Harrington for and with Franza Giffen (Temporary Conservator).
Ms. Rasch withdrew their petition. Ms. Harrington stated that the citation was served on Ms. Averhoff and that they agreed with the Capacity Declaration filed in connection with Ms. Dolch's petition. The Court directed Ms. Harrington to file the citation. Ms. Harrington stated that Ms. Averhoff and Ms. Giffen have a good relationship. Mr. Wild concurred. Ms. Harrington stated the Conservatee was requesting an allowance. The Court granted the petition appointing Franza Giffen as the Conservator of the Person and Estate of Waneta Averhoff with exclusive medical authority (granted subject to review). No dementia powers were granted; no GC 313/335a on file.

Further orders (per written order):
1. Compliance Status Hearing - March 24, 2010, 9:00a.m., Dept. 204:
(A) Filing Of Proof Of Service ("POS") Of Order Appointing Conservator ("OAC"), Notice Of Conservatee's Rights: 30 days from date of OAC;(B) Filing of General Plan: 60 days from date of OAC;(C) Filing of Inventory & Appraisal, Proof of Mailing of Inventory & Appraisal (or Order Dispensing With Same), Service of Notice of How to Object to Inventory & Appraisal, And Proof of Recording Of Letters (If The Estate Includes Real Property): 90 Days From The OAC
2. Assessment Fee - $725 Is Charged. 3. First Account & Status Report - Filed One Week Before February 16, 2011. 4. $250 allowance per week. Bond is $250,000. As to the Trust, Ms. Harrington is to submit a declaration by 1/22/10 as to CRC 7.902/PC 15804 re: notice and is to also submit the second amendment to the Trust. The Trust matter is continued to 2/8/10.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda/Geraldine Anderson Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen.
